data.title: Centurion plans $300,000 private placement
data.description:
• Centurion Minerals (CTN:CA) on Thursday said it plans to raise $300,000 through a non-brokered private placement.
  • The offering will consist of units priced at $0.05 each, with each unit comprising one common share and one warrant exercisable at $0.10 for three years.

  • The company said proceeds will be used for exploration, working capital, and general corporate purposes, with the financing subject to TSX Venture Exchange approval.

  • TMXXF closed +2.54% at $39.12.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sl Green targets 95% year-end same-store occupancy while advancing a $2.5B disposition plan
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: SL Green Realty Corp. (SLG) Q1 2026

Management view

  • "We had the single biggest first quarter in the 28-year history of this company" as SL Green "signed 51 leases totaling 930,000 square feet" with "a mark-to-market that was 16% higher than the previously fully escalated rents on the same spaces," said Chairman & CEO Marc Holliday.
  • "The vacancy rate for trophy buildings dropped again to 3.4%" and Holliday said "there are 0 new space deliveries anticipated for the next 3 years," adding that new projects "343 Madison and 625 Madison" are "not expected to complete until sometime around '29 or 2030."
  • On development, Holliday said SL Green is moving "at a very rapid pace" at 346 Madison, with "100% schematic design on May 1" and expectations to file into ULURP "by the end of this year," with "financial details" planned after pricing and trade feedback.
  • On 750 Third Avenue, Holliday said SL Green has "an agreement with our final remaining tenant for full vacant possession," is "tracking on or below budget by successfully navigating tariffs and inflation," and "hope[s] to finalize our arrangements for debt and equity capital" in coming months.
  • "More to come in the ensuing months as we progress our way through the $2.5 billion disposition plan," Holliday said, also noting SL Green "put out $226 million" via the debt fund since the last call, bringing "total committed to about $567 million" out of a "total $1.3 billion fund."
  • "We announced the promotion of Harrison Sitomer to President and CIO," Holliday said, calling it "a big step towards identifying, growing and supporting the next generation of leaders here."
  • "We increased...our year-end same-store occupancy target from 94.8% to 95%," said CFO Matthew Diliberto.

Outlook

  • "We are forecasting a big boost and shot in the arm...which bodes well for Summit," Holliday said, citing June/July catalysts including "FIFA World Cup" and "the nation's 250th birthday celebrations."
  • Diliberto said SL Green remains aligned with full-year expectations: "As we look out over the balance of the year, we are headed right to the midpoint of our guidance range as well," and added he felt "comfortable about where we are in the guidance range, biased to the higher end versus the lower end of our range."
  • On earnings cadence, Diliberto said quarterly results can be "choppy" due to fee income: "A lot of those fees come in big chunks...Success fees out of special servicing, fees from transactions," and he referenced "DPOs that we still have in our projections for the balance of the year."
  • Compared with last quarter, SL Green’s occupancy target was raised ("from 94.8% to 95%"), and management reiterated longer-dated cash-flow framing, with Holliday stating, "by the time we get to 228, we expect our FAD to be in line with our dividend...and then hopefully and more."

Financial results

  • SL Green reported record leasing volume and spreads in the quarter: "51 leases totaling 930,000 square feet" with "mark-to-market" of "16%," Holliday said.
  • Diliberto disclosed portfolio occupancy metrics: leased occupancy at "94.4%" and economic occupancy at "85.9%," with an end-of-year economic occupancy objective of "89%."
  • Diliberto highlighted same-store cash NOI performance: "That 2.6% positive was 300 basis points higher than what we expected for the first quarter," and said it contributed to his view that results were "biased to the midpoint or higher" of guidance.
  • On dividends, Diliberto said the reset was driven primarily by taxable income and set at "$2.47," adding it "does allow us to retain almost $50 million of incremental capital" for uses including "DPOs, maybe buybacks."

Q&A

  • Steve Sakwa, Evercore ISI: Asked about the "900,000" square-foot pipeline mix and tenant behavior; Chairman & CEO Holliday said the pipeline reflects what’s "available in our portfolio" and cited a goal that "2/3 of our buildings are projected to be...98% or better by the end of this year," while EVP Steven Durels added "30% of that pipeline is leases out" and said "free rent is clearly starting to come down."
  • John Kim, BMO: Asked about upside to occupancy and spreads; CFO Diliberto said, "we increased...our year-end same-store occupancy target...to 95%" and on spreads added, "we typically don't revisit leasing objectives after just 3 months."
  • Alexander Goldfarb, Piper Sandler: Asked whether private credit stress could spread; Chairman & CEO Holliday said, "we're just not seeing it" and referenced the One Madison financing with "44 distinct investors" and "certain classes 7x oversubscribed."
  • Nicholas Yulico, Scotiabank: Asked about no new supply dynamics and FFO cadence; EVP Durels said tenants are doing "early renewals" and cited 1185 Sixth Avenue rents "into the mid-80s to mid-90s a square foot," while CFO Diliberto said Q1 was "not even $0.01 off" internal expectations and noted fee income lumpiness.
  • Anthony Paolone, JPMorgan: Asked about the leased vs. economic occupancy gap and market liquidity; CFO Diliberto said a normalized gap could be "200 basis points" and described dispositions progress, saying, "we have 11 transactions in the business plan" and now "6" dispositions being worked on.
  • Seth Bergey, Citi: Asked about Summit pricing/upsells and buyer profiles/AI; Chairman & CEO Holliday said "Ascent" is the "only upsell," noted "tourism...is off a bit" but said April improved and he expects Summit "ahead of our ambitious targets," while President & CIO Sitomer said investors are "very optimistic" given leasing data.
  • Ronald Kamdem, Morgan Stanley: Pressed on dividend cut rationale and FAD; CFO Diliberto said, "taxable income is what above all else, drives the dividend," and Chairman & CEO Holliday said, "we are leasing the hell out of this portfolio" while acknowledging "there will be a cost to that" in leasing capital.
  • Blaine Heck, Wells Fargo: Asked how sale proceeds align with the $2.5B plan and views on the new mayor; President & CIO Sitomer said proceeds from targeted first-half sales are "approximately half," and Holliday said it is "too early to assess" the administration but added he sees tenants "expanding rather than contracting."
  • Peter Abramowitz, Deutsche Bank: Asked what could drive the high end of guidance and budget-gap solutions; CFO Diliberto cited NOI outperformance and said the fee business is "very, very high margin," while Holliday said the city will reach a balanced budget through "some revenue reforecast and some expense reductions."
  • Vikram Malhotra, Mizuho: Pressed for cash-flow guardrails and Summit expansion timing; Holliday said, "our projections are unchanged" and reiterated the company expects by "2028" to "cover that dividend and more" with FAD, and he said, "We expect to open Paris in summer of '27."
  • Brendan Lynch, Barclays: Asked about lowering cost of debt and buyback triggers; CFO Diliberto said SL Green has "about $3 billion or so left" of a "$7 billion financing plan" for 2026 and expects to benefit as the curve "come[s] down," while Holliday said, "the stock is terribly mispriced" and that with incremental liquidity, "share repurchases are going to get the first and hardest look."

Sentiment analysis

  • Slightly negative analyst tone centered on cash flow, dividends, and visibility, with repeated probing such as Kamdem asking "why not cut the dividend even more" and Malhotra requesting "guardrails" on when leasing gains translate to cash flow.
  • Positive management tone in prepared remarks emphasized record leasing and supply scarcity, with Holliday stating, "I think this was a great quarter" and later adding, "I've never seen a market as good as this one."
  • Management responses in Q&A remained confident and occasionally corrective on framing, including Diliberto saying quarters can be "choppy" and Holliday pushing back on cash-flow timing with "we'll get there probably with that kind of guidance in '27, but not today."
  • Compared with the prior quarter, management maintained a similar emphasis on capital markets access and New York demand, while the analyst focus shifted more directly to dividend mechanics and FAD timing following the dividend reset to $2.47.

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Leasing strength accelerated in Q1, with Holliday calling it the company’s "single biggest first quarter" and reporting 930,000 square feet, while Q4 highlighted "almost 800,000" square feet leased in the quarter and a "pipeline of more than 1 million square feet."
  • Guidance language tightened on occupancy as Diliberto raised the year-end same-store occupancy target to "95%" from "94.8%," whereas Q4 framed 94.8% as the year-end objective.
  • Leadership update was a clear change: Q4 listed Holliday as "Interim President," while Q1 emphasized "the promotion of Harrison Sitomer to President and CIO."
  • Analyst questioning remained heavily oriented to cash flow and dividends in both quarters, but Q1 included more direct buyback discussion, with Holliday stating repurchases would get "the first and hardest look" after business-plan priorities.

Risks and concerns

  • Summit sensitivity to weather and tourism was highlighted, with Diliberto noting "it was a tough weather quarter" and Holliday adding "tourism in the city is off a bit."
  • Cash flow and leasing-capital drag remained a central concern, with management acknowledging continued spend: Holliday said there will be "spend years for the balance of this year and next" as SL Green targets "96%, 97%, 98% occupancy."
  • Development execution and financing remain milestones, with Holliday stating 346 Madison financial details will follow once the project is priced and 750 Third Avenue capital arrangements are expected to be finalized in coming months.

Final takeaway

Management described Q1 as a record leasing quarter, tied to a prime-office supply squeeze and improving net effective rent dynamics, while reaffirming a full-year path toward the midpoint to upper end of guidance. The company raised its year-end same-store occupancy target to 95%, reiterated a $2.5B disposition plan, and outlined major near-term catalysts and milestones across developments (346 Madison and 750 Third) and Summit, while repeatedly positioning dividend policy around taxable income and targeting improved FAD coverage by 2028.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: InvenTrust Properties raises $250M via private placement of senior notes
data.description:
• InvenTrust Properties (IVT) announced on Thursday that it has raised $250M through a private placement of senior unsecured notes, split into three series: Series A ($50M at 5.09% due 2029), Series B ($100M at 5.32% due 2031), and Series C ($100M at 5.60% due 2033).
  • The notes are expected to mature in about 5.4 years and pay a fixed interest rate of ~5.44% on average.
  • No subsidiary guarantees are expected at issuance; guarantees may apply only if required under existing primary credit facilities.
  • The net proceeds from the offering will be used for general corporate purposes, including debt repayment.
  • The notes are expected to be issued on June 29, 2026.
  • The stock traded higher to $32.10 in the after-hours trading session.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Oportun Financial appoints new CEO
data.description:
• Oportun Financial (OPRT) named Doug Bland as chief executive officer and board member, effective April 20, 2026.
  • Bland most recently held senior leadership roles at PayPal, including SVP and GM of its consumer business.
  • He currently serves on the boards of WebBank and Creditly, and will step down from WebBank upon joining Oportun.

data.title: Stocks to watch on Thursday after hours: NFLX, KNX, ARLO
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages closed at new highs on Thursday, a day after the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) had reached records on hopes that the U.S.-Iran war could be over soon.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed +0.3%, while the heavy-tech Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended +0.4%. Both indices marked new all-time high closes of 7,041.20 points and 24,102.70 points, respectively. The Dow (DJI) ended +0.2%.

Here are the 3 stocks to watch on Thursday after hours:

Netflix (NFLX) surrendered its gains during Thursday’s regular hours and slipped into negative territory after better-than-expected first-quarter results were overshadowed by lackluster guidance. The streaming giant reported Q1 sales of $12.25B, up 16% year-over-year, beating Wall Street’s expectations of $12.17B, but maintained its full-year revenue guidance midpoint at $51.2B, below the $51.38B consensus estimate.

Knight-Swift Transportation (KNX) fell 3% in after-hours trading after the trucking company lowered its first-quarter profit outlook to $0.08 to $0.10 per share, down sharply from an earlier range of $0.28 to $0.32 per share. The revision reflects the impact of a large arbitration award from a 2022 incident, higher fuel prices, and unfavorable winter weather conditions.

Arlo Technologies (ARLO) rose 1.35% in after-hours trade to $14.99 after announcing the completion of its acquisition of Aloe Care Health. The deal expands Arlo’s software-as-a-service platform into aging-in-place and wellness care services, adding AI-powered medical alert and fall prevention capabilities targeting the growing population aged 65 and older. Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

data.title: Netflix beats Q1, misses on guidance: Here's how related ETFs have performed YTD
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) shares turned negative as investors looked past a strong Q1 beat and fixated instead on guidance that fell short of expectations for the current quarter and full year.

NFLX stock has gained approximately 18.5% year-to-date through April, a resilience that has been closely tracked by a range of ETFs tied to the streaming giant, from leveraged plays to income-focused products.

The following list ranks Netflix-related ETFs by their year-to-date performance. The list is topped by Direxion Daily NFLX Bull 2X ETF (NFXL), with a YTD performance of 22.25%. Roundhill NFLX WeeklyPay ETF (NFLW) and Truth Social American Icons ETF (TSIC) follow with gains of 4.53% and 3.83%, respectively.

On the lower end of the performance spectrum, several funds have posted negative returns year-to-date. Invesco Next Gen Media and Gaming ETF (GGME) sits at the bottom with a YTD decline of 7.48%, while FT Vest Dow Jones Internet & Target Income ETF (FDND) and REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI) have also struggled with losses.

Here is the list:

  1. Direxion Daily NFLX Bull 2X ETF (NFXL), YTD perf: 22.25%, Quant rating: Hold 3.21

  2. Roundhill NFLX WeeklyPay ETF (NFLW), YTD perf: 4.53%

  3. Truth Social American Icons ETF (TSIC), YTD perf: 3.83%

  4. iShares S&P 500 Communication Sctr UCITS ETF USD A (IVSPF), YTD perf: 0.74%

  5. First Trust Dow Jones Internet Index Fund ETF (FDN), YTD perf: -3.87%, Quant rating: Hold 2.68

  6. REX FANG & Innovation Equity Premium Income ETF (FEPI), YTD perf: -5.77%, Quant rating: Sell 2.39

  7. FT Vest Dow Jones Internet & Target Income ETF (FDND), YTD perf: -5.91%, Quant rating: Not Covered

  8. Invesco Next Gen Media and Gaming ETF (GGME), YTD perf: -7.48%, Quant rating: Sell 2.26

data.title: RTX's unit wins $904.6M contract modification for U.S. Army defense units
data.description:
• Raytheon Missiles and Defense, a unit of RTX (RTX), won a $904.6M contract modification to support low-rate initial production of five Lower Tier Air and Missile Defense System units and six spare units for the U.S. Army.
  • The total contract value now stands at $5.36B.
  • The work will be carried out in Andover, Massachusetts, with completion expected by August 29, 2031.
  • Under Fiscal Year 2026 Army funds, $725.9M was obligated at the time of the award.
  • The contract is managed by Army Contracting Command.

data.title: LifeVantage appoints Moorehead as CEO, effective August
data.description:
• LifeVantage (LFVN) on Thursday said it has appointed Terrence Moorehead as President and Chief Executive Officer, effective August 5, 2026.

  • Moorehead will also join the company’s board and is set to take up the role after completing commitments with his current employer.

  • He will succeed Steve Fife, who is scheduled to retire on April 30, with Director Michael Beindorff serving as interim CEO until Moorehead assumes the position.

  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gold little changed as traders weigh progress toward Middle East truce
data.description:
Gold futures fell slightly in quiet trading Thursday after hitting a one-month intraday high in the previous ‌session on rising optimism that a resolution to the war in Iran looks possible.

President Trump announced that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon reached a 10-day ceasefire deal, after weeks of fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah., and said prospects for a deal with Iran are "looking very good," with the two sides in discussions to extend a ceasefire ahead of its expiration next week.

"If we do ‌see ⁠some type of easing of U.S.-Iran tensions or an end to the war, there will be a stronger likelihood of Federal Reserve rate cuts down the road, [which] could support the precious metals complex," High RIdge Futures director of metals trading David Meger said in a note.

Gold has declined ~9% since the start of the war, as a liquidity squeeze prompted investors to offload holdings and cover losses elsewhere; however, in a sign that buyers are coming back to the market, bullion-backed ETFs have added ~25 tons so far this month, after cutting 94 tons in March, according to a Bloomberg tally.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery closed down 0.3% to $4,785.40/oz, and front-month Comex April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) slipped 1.1% to $78.606/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (DUST), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)
